quick_facts.label: Current national curriculum nomenclature
quick_facts.value: The NCAHP competency-based curriculum calls the baccalaureate programme “B.AOTT” (Baccalaureate in Anaesthesia & Operation Theatre Technology). Institutions may still use titles such as B.Sc. in Operation Theatre Technology; applicants should confirm the exact award title with the university.

quick_facts.label: Standard entry background
quick_facts.value: The NCAHP B.AOTT curriculum specifies 10+2/equivalent with at least 50% in Physics, Chemistry and Biology; Mathematics is optional in that curriculum. Institution-specific requirements can be more restrictive.

quick_facts.label: Standard programme pattern
quick_facts.value: The NCAHP baccalaureate curriculum lays out six academic semesters followed by a one-year internship in anaesthesia, surgery and specialty postings—normally a four-year pathway in total.

quick_facts.label: Professional scope
quick_facts.value: Training is directed toward supervised perioperative support: preparing and maintaining the operating theatre, assisting anaesthesia and surgical teams, monitoring infection-control procedures, supporting recovery care, and handling relevant equipment within assigned competence and institutional protocols.

quick_facts.label: National standards context
quick_facts.value: NCAHP’s competency-based curriculum for Anaesthesia and Operation Theatre Technology is among the allied-health curricula identified for mandatory implementation from academic year 2026–27.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: For the current NCAHP baccalaureate curriculum in Anaesthesia & Operation Theatre Technology, the baseline entry qualification is Higher Secondary/10+2 or equivalent from a recognised board with 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry and Biology; Mathematics is listed as optional. The curriculum also addresses equivalence for international qualifications through the Association of Indian Universities.

Individual universities can set additional conditions. For example, DIT University’s 2026–27 B.AOTT listing requires 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Mathematics, sets a minimum age of 17 by 31 December of the admission year, and requires a government medical officer-endorsed fitness certificate. Check the prospectus for the specific institution and admission cycle rather than treating one college’s rules as national rules.
bullets: Recommended preparation: strong Class 12 foundations in PCB, comfort with clinical learning, careful adherence to patient-safety and infection-control processes, and readiness for supervised hospital postings.
Verify whether the course accepts PCB only or PCB/Mathematics, its required percentage, age and medical-fitness conditions, domicile/category rules, and whether it offers any lateral-entry route.

sections.heading: Duration and clinical training
paragraphs: Under the NCAHP baccalaureate curriculum, the academic component spans the first six semesters, followed by a compulsory one-year internship labelled as seventh and eighth semesters. The internship is structured across anaesthesia, critical care and multiple surgical/OT settings; the curriculum describes it as 12 months, with six months in anaesthesia and six months in surgical disciplines.

Programme duration is not uniform across every legacy B.Sc. OTT offering. Older institutional syllabi may show three academic years plus internship, 3.5 years, or other structures. For admissions from 2026–27, confirm that the programme’s duration, internship and degree nomenclature align with the applicable NCAHP framework and the university’s approved regulations.
bullets: NCAHP internship examples include anaesthesia, critical care, general surgery/obstetrics and gynaecology, urology and paediatric surgery, CTVS/neurosurgery, emergency or trauma OT/orthopaedics, robotic surgery, and minor surgery.
Keep a clinical logbook: the national curriculum requires documented postings and faculty/senior review during internship.

sections.heading: What students study and learn
paragraphs: This is an allied-health, perioperative-care degree rather than a medical degree. The NCAHP curriculum aims to prepare graduates to assist anaesthesiologists during procedures and patient monitoring; work with operating-theatre technology and biomedical equipment; support pre-operative, intra-operative, recovery-room and post-operative care; and maintain infection-control standards while working under doctors’ guidance and supervision.

Coursework combines biomedical foundations with applied OT and anaesthesia practice. The NCAHP structure includes anatomy and physiology, biochemistry and haematology, pathology and microbiology, pharmacology, medical law and ethics, management, communication and computing. Professional modules cover basic and advanced anaesthesia techniques, surgical procedures, CSSD and manifold-area work, intensive care, specialised anaesthesia and surgery, disaster/environmental management, research methodology and biostatistics.

Practical and clinical learning are central. The curriculum specifies clinical/practical components alongside theory and then a full-year internship. Students should evaluate whether a prospective college has sufficient OT exposure, anaesthesia and critical-care postings, simulation/laboratory facilities, named hospital affiliations, and a transparent supervision and logbook process.
bullets: Core technical themes: anaesthesia support, surgical instruments and procedures, OT preparation and equipment, medical gases/manifold systems, sterilisation/CSSD, patient positioning and transfer, infection prevention, safety, documentation and biomedical-waste handling.

Professional boundary: graduates assist the clinical team within their training, assigned role and institutional protocols; diagnosis, prescribing and independent medical decision-making are not the purpose of this qualification.

sections.heading: Admission route
paragraphs: The NCAHP curriculum describes admission based on eligibility plus a university entrance test and interview, followed by merit-based counselling/seat allotment. In actual practice, selection mechanisms differ by institution: some use university entrance tests, while others publish 10+2-merit admissions. Therefore, applicants should treat the relevant university’s admission notice as controlling.

Prepare a document file early: Class 10 and 12 marksheets/certificates, photo ID, category or domicile documents where applicable, photographs, medical-fitness documentation if required, and equivalence documents for non-Indian qualifications. Confirm the 2026–27 application opening date, test/interview schedule, fee-payment deadline and hostel process directly with the institution.
bullets: Examples of variation: the NCAHP curriculum sets out an entrance-test-and-interview model, while DIT University states admission is on Senior Secondary merit for its B.AOTT course.

Before paying an application fee, verify the awarding university, exact degree title, programme duration including internship, clinical-training hospital, approved intake, refund rules and total compulsory charges.

sections.heading: Fees: dated examples, not a national benchmark
paragraphs: Fees vary materially by state, institution type, quota, scholarships, hostel choice and charges outside tuition. As dated 2026–27 examples, DIT University lists two instalments of ₹75,150 each for its All-India B.AOTT first year (₹150,300 before separately listed admission and caution-money charges). MGM School of Biomedical Sciences, Navi Mumbai lists a first-year total of ₹144,500 for its Anaesthesia and Operation Theatre Technology allied-health programme, followed by listed annual totals of ₹114,000, ₹114,000 and ₹114,500 for years two through four.

These examples are provider-specific published schedules, not an estimate of the cost of a B.Sc. OTT nationwide. Ask each college for a written fee sheet covering tuition, university/enrolment fees, examination/practical charges, refundable deposits, internship charges if any, transport, hostel/mess and annual increases. Check eligibility and continuation rules before relying on a scholarship.
bullets: DIT’s displayed 2026–27 first-year schedule separately lists a non-refundable ₹10,000 admission fee and a refundable ₹5,000 caution-money charge in addition to the displayed programme fee.

MGM’s displayed 2026–27 first-year total includes tuition, institute enrolment/eligibility fee, refundable deposit and semester examination fees; it should not be compared with tuition-only figures without adjustment.

sections.heading: After the degree
paragraphs: Typical entry employment is in hospital operating theatres, anaesthesia departments, recovery areas, critical-care units and trauma centres, as part of a multidisciplinary team. The NCAHP curriculum describes graduates as working under doctors’ guidance and supervision and identifies hospital and independent trauma-centre opportunities; the specific job title, duties and hiring requirements vary by employer and state.

For academic progression, the NCAHP curriculum includes a Master’s in Anaesthesia & Operation Theatre Technology (M.AOTT). Its stated focus is advanced knowledge in planning, setting up, maintaining and managing anaesthesia and surgical procedures, equipment handling, clinical supervision, research and training. Admission requirements must be checked with the university offering the master’s programme.

Build employability through a complete internship logbook, documented OT/anaesthesia/critical-care rotations, infection-control and patient-safety competence, equipment familiarity, communication skills and continuing professional education. Registration, recognition and recruitment rules can evolve as NCAHP standards are implemented, so verify the applicable state and employer requirements at the time of application.
bullets: Possible early-career titles may include operation theatre technologist, anaesthesia technologist or surgical/anaesthesia-related technology staff; nomenclature is employer-dependent.

A master’s degree and subsequent experience can support progression toward more senior clinical, teaching, supervision, management, research or industry-facing roles, subject to the employer’s criteria.
